name: Ayuda Venezuela
db_description:
**¿Qué construiste?**
Un bot de Telegram en español —@DEV_VEZbot— que les da a los venezolanos información de emergencia clara (refugios, agua segura, salud, ayuda humanitaria) con solo chatear en español sencillo. Sin app, sin registro.

Por dentro es un agente de IA construido sobre surfcall (de Gecko): una capa que hace que cualquier API sea utilizable por un agente de IA sin escribir código de integración. surfcall lee la API de SOS Venezuela 2026, la convierte en las herramientas adecuadas y hace la llamada correcta al primer intento, así las respuestas vienen de datos en vivo, no de suposiciones.

**¿Para quién es?**
Para cualquier persona en Venezuela que necesite información de emergencia rápido y no sea desarrollador, llegando a donde ya está (Telegram), en el idioma que habla (español). Y para el proyecto SOS Venezuela: una puerta de entrada sin fricción a sus datos.

**¿Cómo ayuda?**

* Convierte una API para desarrolladores en algo que una persona común puede simplemente preguntar.
* Respuestas en tiempo real a partir de los datos de SOS Venezuela 2026.
* Gratis, sin instalación, sin registro: cualquier teléfono con Telegram.
* Código abierto (MIT). También aportamos mejoras a la API de SOS Venezuela (una especificación OpenAPI + sugerencias de endpoints) para que otros también puedan construir sobre ella.
* Reutilizable para cualquier fuente: trae una API o un conjunto de datos y lo hacemos utilizable por agentes: con un clic si tiene OpenAPI, o lo integramos a mano. SOS Venezuela es solo la primera.

**¿Qué deberían probar primero?**
Abre t.me/DEV_VEZbot y pregunta, en español:

* "¿Dónde hay un refugio cerca de mí?"
* "¿El agua de mi zona es segura para tomar?"
* "¿Cómo pido o doy ayuda?"

analysis.summary: A Spanish-first Telegram bot that acts as an AI agent (Claude/OpenRouter tool-use loop) over the public read-only 'SOS Venezuela 2026' earthquake-response API, using the author's own published 'gecko-surf' library to auto-generate typed tools from the API's OpenAPI spec. Paired with a Next.js marketing landing page. The bot itself is a thin, well-tested, defensively-coded consumer layer rather than the data platform (which lives in a separate repo).
analysis.project_type: Telegram bot (AI agent) + Next.js marketing landing page

ai_ml: OpenRouter free LLM (Llama 3.3 70B) as default provider

architecture.pattern: Thin consumer/adapter service — Telegram transport layer -> agent tool-use loop -> allow-listed API tool wrapper (SurfcallTools) -> external SOS Venezuela public API via gecko-surf client. Separate, decoupled Next.js static landing page.
architecture.orm_or_db_layer: None — no database; the bot is stateless (in-memory per-process rate limiter only) and reads from a remote read-only public API via the gecko-surf client library.
architecture.api_design: No inbound HTTP API from the bot itself (long-polling Telegram, no open ports); an OpenAPI spec for the upstream SOS Venezuela API is vendored at bot/spec/sosvenezuela_openapi.json and consumed via gecko-surf. Landing page is a static Next.js app.
architecture.separation_of_concerns: Clean: bot.py (Telegram transport, pure/testable handlers), agent.py (LLM tool-use loop, provider-agnostic), surfcall_tools.py (safety boundary: allow-list, sanitize, cap), providers.py (Anthropic/OpenRouter adapter), config.py (env-only secrets, system prompt).
architecture.modularity: High for its scope — small, single-responsibility modules with dependency injection (responder/clock/llm/tools all injectable) enabling offline unit testing without network or real credentials.
notable_patterns: Explicit read-only tool allow-list (5 named operations) as a prompt-injection/safety boundary, enforced in code not just prompt
Provider abstraction shimming OpenAI-compatible (OpenRouter) responses into the Anthropic Messages shape so one agent loop works with either provider
Never-raises design at every boundary (tool calls, message handling) degrading to a friendly Spanish fallback instead of crashing
Bounded agent loop (max_iters) to prevent infinite tool-call loops
Structural JSON truncation (_cap) that drops trailing list items to stay under a char budget while remaining valid JSON, rather than naive byte-truncation
Per-user sliding-window rate limiter to protect upstream API budget and LLM spend

code_organization.directory_structure: Clear top-level split: bot/ (Python agent), app/components/lib (Next.js landing), tests/, .github/workflows — logical and small enough to navigate instantly.
code_organization.naming_quality: Consistent, descriptive names (SurfcallTools, RateLimiter, handle_message, resolve_command) with Spanish user-facing strings clearly segregated as constants.
code_organization.documentation_quality: Strong module-level docstrings explaining intent/design rationale in every bot/*.py file plus a detailed bilingual README and a bot/README.md; comments explain 'why' not just 'what'.
code_organization.reasoning: Small but exceptionally well-organized codebase for its size, with docstrings that explicitly document design decisions and safety rationale — well above typical hackathon quality.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: Secrets read only from environment variables (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, OPENROUTER_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); .env is gitignored; error messages name only the missing var, never a value, to avoid leaking secrets.
production_readiness.security_notes: No end-user auth needed since it only reads a public no-auth API; the real security control is the code-enforced 5-operation allow-list plus an explicit prompt-injection defense (tool results are data, not instructions) and privacy masking of cédulas/coords/minors deferred to the upstream API. No rate limiting beyond per-user in-memory (resets on restart, not distributed) and no logging/observability for abuse detection or incident response.
production_readiness.scalability_notes: Long-polling single-process Telegram bot with in-memory rate-limit state — fine for hackathon/demo scale but won't horizontally scale or survive restarts gracefully (state loss); no persistence, no queue, no DB. Landing page is on Vercel (static, scales fine).
production_readiness.reasoning: Docker image, CI (lint/type/test for Python + lint/build for Next.js), and defensive error handling are real production hygiene, but there is no logging/observability, no persistent state, no horizontal-scale story, and it depends entirely on an external sibling API/library ('SOS Venezuela 2026', 'gecko-surf') whose own production-readiness is out of scope/unknown.

maturity.is_real_or_boilerplate: Real, working logic — not boilerplate. Small (~1250 LOC Python across bot+tests) but functionally complete for its narrow scope, with genuine unit tests exercising rate limiting, command routing, message chunking, the tool allow-list, and JSON capping.
maturity.reasoning: 22 commits, essentially one real developer (plus Vercel/v0 bot commits for the landing scaffold), built in ~2 days around a hackathon; CI actually lints/type-checks/tests both the Python bot and Next.js landing, which is more rigor than most hackathon repos show.

viability.reasoning: Functionally it is a working MVP with real tests and defensible safety design, but it delivers value only by proxy of a separate, unverified 'SOS Venezuela 2026' data platform and a personal PyPI package (gecko-surf) that this review did not audit for correctness/uptime; without that upstream API being genuinely live and reliable, the bot has nothing to answer with, capping standalone viability for disaster response.

key_features: Spanish-first (auto-detects and replies in the user's language) Telegram bot for querying earthquake emergency data
AI agent tool-use loop with pluggable LLM provider (free OpenRouter Llama 3.3 70B by default, Claude Haiku 4.5 opt-in)
Hard-coded allow-list of 5 read-only operations (search persons, get reports, person stats, recent damage, news) as a security boundary
Telegram message chunking for long replies, command shortcuts (/buscar, /cifras, /reportes, /noticias)
Structural JSON response capping to keep large API payloads within LLM context while remaining valid JSON
Dockerized bot worker; separate Next.js marketing landing page deployed to Vercel
analysis.merge_potential: Could be merged/integrated with other Build4Venezuela repos as the conversational/Telegram front-end layer, provided it points at a shared canonical 'SOS Venezuela' data API; its OpenAPI spec and allow-list pattern could be reused by other bots/agents needing safe read-only access to the same humanitarian data.
red_flags: Entire product value depends on an external, unaudited API ('SOS Venezuela 2026') and a personally-published PyPI dependency ('gecko-surf') from the same author — single point of failure and unverified reliability/maintenance
No logging/observability or persistent rate-limit/audit state — a restart silently resets abuse protection and there's no way to detect misuse or outages in production
Single-maintainer project (one real human contributor) built in ~2 days — bus-factor and long-term maintenance risk for a tool meant to serve real disaster victims
No authentication/authorization needed only because the bot is read-only against a public API; if that upstream API's own access controls are weak, this bot inherits that risk

diffusion.recommended_angle: Pregúntale a un bot de Telegram en español dónde hay refugio o si el agua es segura para tomar — sin descargar apps, sin registrarte, solo escribe a @DEV_VEZbot.
gaps: No independent verification that the actual Telegram bot (@DEV_VEZbot) responds correctly in real time — only the landing page was checked live
Entire value depends on an external, unaudited 'SOS Venezuela 2026' API and the author's own PyPI package (gecko-surf), a single point of failure
No screenshots of the actual chat conversation included in assets; only a landing page and video
Single-maintainer project built in ~2 days with no logging/observability, so misuse or downtime would go undetected

evaluation.live_demo_notes: Landing page at https://ayuda-venezuela-wine.vercel.app/ loads correctly and is a real, presentable marketing page (not broken/boilerplate). It explains the value prop clearly, links directly to the live Telegram bot (@DEV_VEZbot), links to GitHub, and explains the underlying 'gecko-surf' agent tooling. The actual bot conversation flow itself was not tested via chat (WebFetch only reviewed the landing page), so end-to-end victim usability is unverified but plausible given the codebase's genuine unit tests around message handling.
evaluation.one_line_pitch: Ayuda Venezuela: escríbele a un bot de Telegram en español y obtén al instante refugios, agua segura y ayuda humanitaria — sin apps, sin registro.
evaluation.real_problem_reasoning: Access to timely, low-friction emergency information (shelter locations, water safety, humanitarian aid contacts) is a genuine, urgent need for earthquake victims, and reaching people where they already are (Telegram, in plain Spanish, no app install/registration) removes real adoption barriers compared to a dedicated app or web dashboard. However, the project is a thin conversational wrapper around a separate 'SOS Venezuela 2026' data API that this review did not independently verify for liveness/accuracy — so it solves the access/UX problem but its life-saving value is fully contingent on data it doesn't own or control.
